title: Variant 1 - Sports Club and Reading
description: Official-style speaking practice variant based on client materials.

task1.title: Read Aloud
task1.readingText: The ninth planet of the solar system was discovered not long ago. It happened in 1930. Scientists had been hunting for the planet for a long time. They had calculated its probable position but there was no proof that the planet really existed. It was too far away for the telescopes of that time to find it. It's worth mentioning that the first photos of the planet were taken by a very young researcher. He was only twenty-four and had no formal education in astronomy. However, he was deeply involved in the search for the ninth planet. The planet at the edge of the solar system was called Pluto, after the Roman god. The name for the planet was suggested by an 11-year-old British girl.
task1.referenceText: The ninth planet of the solar system was discovered not long ago. It happened in 1930. Scientists had been hunting for the planet for a long time. It's worth mentioning that the first photos of the planet were taken by a very young researcher. He was only twenty-four and had no formal education in astronomy. However, he was deeply involved in the search for the ninth planet. The planet at the edge of the solar system was called Pluto, after the Roman god. The name for the planet was suggested by an 11-year-old British girl.

questions.text: How many times a week do you do sports?
questions.referenceText: I do sports three times a week.

questions.text: What sport is the most popular with teenagers in your region?
questions.referenceText: Football is the most popular sport among teenagers in my region. A lot of my friends play it and watch matches.

questions.text: What sports facilities are available in the place where you live?
questions.referenceText: In my town, we have a stadium, a swimming pool, and several gyms. There are also basketball courts and football fields.

questions.text: Why do you think it is important to keep fit?
questions.referenceText: I think it is important to keep fit because it helps you stay healthy, feel more energetic and be in a good mood. Sport also helps to reduce stress.

questions.text: What would you advise a person who wants to keep fit?
questions.referenceText: I would advise them to do sports regularly, eat healthy food and get enough sleep. It's also important to spend time outdoors and avoid bad habits.

task3.title: Monologue
plan: what kind of books modern teenagers enjoy reading
whether libraries are necessary nowadays or not, and why
what book you have read recently, and what it was about
what your attitude to reading is
task3.referenceText: Nowadays, modern teenagers often prefer fantasy, science fiction, and dystopian novels. These genres captivate with their imaginative worlds, fast-paced plots, and relatable young characters. Many also enjoy young adult fiction that explores friendship, identity, and self-discovery. As for libraries, I believe they are still very necessary. They provide free access to a wide range of books, which is especially important for students and those who cannot afford to buy many books. Libraries also offer a quiet space for study and host various cultural events. Recently, I read 1984 by George Orwell. This classic dystopian novel tells the story of a totalitarian society where the government controls every aspect of life. It made me think deeply about freedom, truth, and the power of information. As for my attitude to reading, I think it is extremely important. Reading broadens our horizons, improves vocabulary, and helps us understand different perspectives. It is not only a source of knowledge but also a great way to relax and escape from everyday routine.

title: Environmental Awareness
description: Speaking test to assess candidate's awareness of environmental issues

task1.title: Read Aloud
task1.readingText: The concept of climate change has been a pressing issue for decades. It refers to the long-term warming of the planet due to an increase in average global temperatures. This phenomenon is primarily caused by the increasing levels of greenhouse gases in the Earth's atmosphere, which trap heat from the sun. Human activities such as burning fossil fuels, deforestation, and industrial processes are major contributors to the rise in greenhouse gas emissions. The effects of climate change are widespread, ranging from more frequent natural disasters like hurricanes and wildfires to rising sea levels and altered ecosystems. It is essential for individuals, organizations, and governments to work together to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and mitigate the impacts of climate change.
task1.referenceText: The concept of climate change has been a pressing issue for decades. It refers to the long-term warming of the planet due to an increase in average global temperatures. This phenomenon is primarily caused by the increasing levels of greenhouse gases in the Earth's atmosphere, which trap heat from the sun. Human activities such as burning fossil fuels, deforestation, and industrial processes are major contributors to the rise in greenhouse gas emissions. The effects of climate change are widespread, ranging from more frequent natural disasters like hurricanes and wildfires to rising sea levels and altered ecosystems. It is essential for individuals, organizations, and governments to work together to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and mitigate the impacts of climate change.

questions.text: What do you think is the most significant environmental issue facing our planet today?
questions.referenceText: Climate change is the most significant environmental issue facing our planet today.

questions.text: How often do you use public transport or walk/bike instead of driving?
questions.referenceText: I use public transport or walk/bike at least 3 times a week.

questions.text: Do you think recycling is an effective way to reduce waste?
questions.referenceText: Yes, I think recycling is an effective way to reduce waste.

questions.text: What do you think is the most important step individuals can take to reduce their carbon footprint?
questions.referenceText: Reducing energy consumption by using energy-efficient appliances and turning off lights when not in use is the most important step individuals can take.

questions.text: Do you think governments should implement policies to reduce greenhouse gas emissions?
questions.referenceText: Yes, I think governments should implement policies to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

questions.text: How important is environmental sustainability to you when making purchasing decisions?
questions.referenceText: Environmental sustainability is very important to me when making purchasing decisions.

task3.title: Monologue
plan: Explain the impact of plastic waste on the environment
Discuss ways to reduce plastic waste, such as using reusable bags and water bottles
Describe the importance of recycling and proper waste disposal
Propose a plan to implement a plastic waste reduction initiative in your community
task3.referenceText: Reducing plastic waste is a crucial step towards mitigating the harmful effects of pollution on our environment. Plastic waste, if not disposed of properly, can end up in oceans and waterways, harming marine life and contaminating the food chain. To reduce plastic waste, individuals can take simple steps such as using reusable bags and water bottles, avoiding products with excessive packaging, and recycling as much as possible. Furthermore, communities can implement initiatives such as beach cleanups, recycling programs, and education campaigns to raise awareness about the importance of reducing plastic waste. By working together, we can significantly reduce the amount of plastic waste that ends up in our environment and promote a healthier, more sustainable future.
